nl: create a correlation matrix from mtcars using cor(), reshape it into a long format, then plot it as a heatmap tile chart
expected_code:
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)
cor_mat <- cor(mtcars)
cor_long <- melt(cor_mat)
ggplot(cor_long, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = value)) + geom_tile()

nl: using mtcars with cyl as a factor, compute the mean and standard deviation of mpg for each cylinder count, then plot the means as points with error bars for the standard deviation
expected_code:
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
mtcars %>% group_by(cyl) %>% summarise(mean_mpg = mean(mpg), sd_mpg = sd(mpg)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(cyl), y = mean_mpg)) + geom_point() + geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_mpg - sd_mpg, ymax = mean_mpg + sd_mpg), width = 0.2)

nl: compute the arithmetic mean of the mpg column in mtcars
get a summary of all columns in the mtcars dataset
fit a linear regression model predicting mpg from hp using the mtcars dataset
create a frequency table showing the count of each cylinder value in mtcars
compute the mean mpg for each cylinder group in mtcars using aggregate
expected_code: aggregate(mpg ~ cyl, data = mtcars, FUN = mean)

nl: run a two-sample t-test comparing mpg between cars with automatic and manual transmission in mtcars
compute the Pearson correlation coefficient between mpg and hp in mtcars
using base R graphics, make a scatter plot of mpg on the x-axis and hp on the y-axis from mtcars
using base R graphics, make a histogram of the mpg values from mtcars
using base R graphics, make a side-by-side boxplot of mpg for each cylinder count in mtcars
from mtcars, extract only the rows where mpg is greater than 20 using base R subsetting
create two small data frames, df1=data.frame(id=1:3, a=letters[1:3]) and df2=data.frame(id=2:4, b=letters[2:4]), then merge them by the id column
expected_code:
df1 <- data.frame(id = 1:3, a = letters[1:3], stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
df2 <- data.frame(id = 2:4, b = letters[2:4], stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
merge(df1, df2, by = "id")

nl: compute the column means for the numeric columns of mtcars using apply
apply the summary function to each column of iris using lapply
sort the mpg values from mtcars in ascending order
compute the 25th, 50th, and 75th percentiles of mpg from mtcars
standardize the mpg column of mtcars by centering to zero mean and scaling to unit variance
run a one-way ANOVA comparing mpg across different cylinder counts in mtcars
fit a logistic regression model predicting transmission type from mpg in mtcars
expected_code: glm(am ~ mpg, data = mtcars, family = binomial)
